analogy: Think of "Recent Developments in Indian Politics" through the chapter's own opening images: In this chapter… In this last chapter we take a synoptic view of the last two decades of politics in India. These developments are complex, for various kinds of factors came together to produce unanticipated outcomes in this period. The new era in politics was... The easy idea is that the visible surface is only the outside cover. The chapter keeps asking how we can know what is inside when no one can directly reach the deep interior. So the learner must read clues: rocks, mines, drilling, volcanic material, heat, gravity, magnetism and earthquake waves. Like solving a hidden-layer puzzle, each clue tells something about the crust, mantle, core and volcanic activity without inventing anything beyond the textbook.
full_notes:
Recent Developments in Indian Politics is decoded here only from the NCERT chapter text. The chapter begins by asking how the nature of the earth should be imagined and why the interior cannot be known in the same direct way as the surface. It connects the hidden interior with visible results on the surface, especially landscape development, earthquakes, tsunamis and volcanic activity.

The text explains that in this chapter… In this last chapter we take a synoptic view of the last two decades of politics in India.

The text explains that these developments are complex, for various kinds of factors came together to produce unanticipated outcomes in this period.

The text explains that the new era in politics was impossible to foresee; it is still very difficult to understand.

The text explains that these developments are also controversial, for these involve deep conflicts and we are still too close to the events.

The text explains that yet we can ask some questions central to the political change in this period. • What are the implications of the rise of coalition politics for our democracy? • What is Mandalisation all about?

The text explains that in which ways will it change the nature of political representation? • What is the legacy of the Ram Janmabhoomi movement? • What does the rise of a new policy consensus do to the nature of political choices?

The text explains that it simply gives you the necessary information and some tools so that you can ask and answer these questions when you are through with this book.

The text explains that we cannot avoid asking these questions just because they are politically sensitive, for the whole point of studying the history of politics in India since Independence is to make sense of our present.

The text explains that ups and downs of various political parties in the 1990s appeared to many, like this cartoon drawn in 1990, as a roller coaster ride.

The text explains that riding the roller coaster are Rajiv Gandhi, V.

The text explains that context of the 1990s You have read in the last chapter that Rajiv Gandhi became the Prime Minister after the assassination of Indira Gandhi.

The text explains that he led the Congress to a massive victory in the Lok Sabha elections held immediately thereafter in 1984.

The text explains that as the decade of the eighties came to a close, the country witnessed five developments that were to make a long-lasting impact on our politics.

The text explains that f irst the most crucial development of this period was the defeat of the Congress party in the elections held in 1989.

The text explains that the party that had won as many as 415 seats in the Lok Sabha in 1984 was reduced to only 197 in this election.

The text explains that the Congress improved its performance and came back to power soon after the mid-term elections held in 1991.

The text explains that started by Rajiv Gandhi, these changes first became very visible in 1991 and radically changed the direction that the Indian economy had pursued since Independence.

The text explains that the Ram Janmabhoomi Temple Movement, becoming the central issue, transformed the direction of the discourse on secularism and democracy.

The text explains that karpoori Thakur, the then Chief Minister of Bihar, was a pioneer in this direction.

The text explains that after 1986, the party began to emphasise nationalism as the core of its ideology.

The text explains that recent Developments in Indian Politics 149 structure was built at the site of Shri Ram’s birth place in 1528, but the structure had visible display of Hindu symbols and relics in its interior as well as its exterior portions.

The text explains that the verdict allotted the disputed site to the Shri Ram Janmabhoomi Teertha Kshetra Trust for the construction of Ram temple and directed the concerned government to allot appropriate site for the construction of a Mosque to the Sunni Central Waqf Board.

The text explains that recent Developments in Indian Politics 155 Party Position in 17th Lok Sabha Bharatiya Janata Party (BJP) 56% Others 20% Bahujan Samaj Party (BSP) 2% Dravida Munnetra Kazhagam (DMK) 4% Shiv Sena (SS) 3% Janata Dal United (JD-U) 3% Biju Janata Dal (BJD) 2% Indian National Congress (INC) 10% Source: http://loksabha.nic.in Bharatiya Janata Party (BJP) 303 Indian National Congress (INC) 52 Dravida Munnetra Kazhagam (DMK) 24 Shiv Sena (SS) 18 Janata Dal (United) [JD(U)] 16 Biju Janata Dal (BJD) 12 Bahujan Samaj Party (BSP) 10 Other Parties 108

The text explains that not only has the Congress system destroyed itself, but the fragmentation of the Congress coalition has triggered a new emphasis on self-representation which raises questions about the party system and its capacity to accommodate diverse interests, …. .

The text explains that an important test facing the polity is to evolve a party system or political parties that can effectively articulate and aggregate a variety of interests. — Zoya Hasan (a) Write a short note on what the author calls challenges of the party system in the light of what you have read in this chapter.

The text explains that (b) Given an example from this chapter of the lack of accommodation and aggregation mentioned in this passage.

The text explains that (c) Why is it necessary for parties to accommodate and aggregate variety of interests?

The text explains that recent Developments in Indian Politics 157 LET US DO IT TOGETHER • This chapter covers the major developments in Indian politics until the 2004 Elections (14th Lok Sabha).

The text explains that subsequently, the Lok Sabha elections were held in 2009, during which the UPA led by the Congress won.

The text explains that in the 2014 and 2019 Elections, the NDA led by the BJP emerged victorious.

The text explains that the position of various parties in the 17th Lok Sabha is given on page 155. • A detailed study of Members of the 17th Lok Sabha is available on the website of the Lok Sabha (http://loksabha.nic.in). • Compare and contrast the electoral performances of various political parties since 2004.

The text explains that you can also collect the data about the results from the website of the Election Commission of India (http://eci.nic.in). • Prepare a timeline of the major political events in India since 2004.

prelims_traps: Do not treat 138 Politics in India since Independence and 1 May 1996 25 October 1995 20 August 2001 25 October 2004 as identical unless the source text links them directly.
Do not add outside examples to Recent Developments in Indian Politics; use only the examples and processes named in the chapter.
Do not confuse a visible surface result with the internal process or source evidence described by the text.
mains_angles: Explain Recent Developments in Indian Politics through the source chain: evidence, process and visible result.
Use 138 Politics in India since Independence and 1 May 1996 25 October 1995 20 August 2001 25 October 2004 to show how the chapter connects facts with mechanisms.
Frame an answer around causes, internal working, surface expression and source limitations from the NCERT chapter.

But the elections of 1989 marked the end of what political scientists have called the ‘Congress system’.
